blockingIssues.description: Address column uses `row.id` in `web/components/transactions-table.tsx` instead of participant address, so required address behavior is not met.
Mobile menu `ConnectButton` resolves to default `Button` height `h-9` (36px), below the required 44px touch target at 375px.
Chart updates are still REST-polled via `useCandles` and do not subscribe to WebSocket data, contrary to feature requirement.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/user-testing.md` to reflect new development mock-candle fallback so chart line/crosshair assertions are testable without seeding in dev mode.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Normalize frontend skill naming/runtime availability and enforce that `features.json` only references installed skills; also reconcile frontend skill validator procedure (web-scoped + build requirement) with accepted mission verification flow.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews reported `frontend-ui-worker` references while runtime required fallback to `frontend-worker`, and reviewers observed validator-procedure mismatch between skill instructions and recorded handoffs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit mobile touch-target minimum (44px) to core conventions/checklist so shared controls (like wallet connect) are not missed during responsive passes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Validation contract requires 44px minimum touch targets; review found mobile nav Connect Wallet control remained 36px due shared button defaults.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align feature requirements with backend API contract for trades address fields (or specify approved fallback) before assigning Address-column assertions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Transactions feature requires Address display, while `/trades` API currently returns `{ id, price, quantity, side, timestamp }` without participant address fields.

rejectedObservations.observation: Duplicate report that `frontend-ui-worker` was unavailable and replaced by `frontend-worker`.
